$schema: Query templates for Memory Benchmark — capability × fact × phrasing variations

direct_recall.description: Retrieve stored facts via direct questions

queries.query: What do I do for a living?
Where did I go to college?
What package manager do I use?
What branch of military did I serve in?

semantic_search.description: Find related facts through indirect phrasing
semantic_search.note: min_expected=1: success if semantic search connects at least 1 fact.

queries.query: Tell me about my tech stack
How do I like to work?
What do I do on weekends?
What do I use for backend?
Tell me about my weekend hobbies
How does our dev team operate?
What are my monthly fixed expenses?
What project management tools do we use?
What was my school life like?
What did I do between college graduation and Naver?
How do you manage code quality?
Tell me about our service status

proactive_recall.description: Apply remembered facts without being asked directly

queries.query: Help me set up my editor config
Let's build a new API server
Refactor this code: function hello() { return 'hi' }
Create a config file for a new project
How do I install a package?
Design a schema for storing data
Set up cloud resources for me
How do I add a new package?
I want to add vector search
Let's set up a monorepo structure
Set up a bug reporting system
I want to add a logging system to the server
I need to pay for something, which card should I use?
Need to set up team email
I want to set up A/B testing
Let me write a tech blog post
I need to edit some photos

abstention.description: Refuse to fabricate facts that were never mentioned (hallucination prevention). FAIL = automatic F grade.

queries.query: What did I say about Kubernetes?
Did I say I use AWS?
What kind of car do I have?
Did I say I speak Japanese?
Did I say I use Nuxt.js?
queries.note: Uses Next.js, NOT Nuxt.js — similar name distinction

queries.query: I mentioned I like tea, right? Green tea?
Did I say I go swimming?
I use Ruby on Rails, right?
Did I say I play guitar?
I run a YouTube channel, right?
Did I say I use Angular?
Did I say I'm learning to surf?
I have a master's degree, right?
Did I say I use MongoDB?

irrelevant_isolation.description: Avoid unnecessarily surfacing memories for unrelated questions

queries.query: How does list comprehension work in Python?
What does HTTP status code 404 mean?
How do you use Promise.all in JavaScript?
What's the difference between TCP and UDP?
What's the tallest mountain in the world?
How do I use CSS flexbox?
What's Schrodinger's equation in quantum mechanics?
How do I use chmod in Linux?
What types of SQL JOINs are there?
What's the difference between React and Vue?
What's the difference between git rebase and merge?
How do coroutines work in Kotlin?

multi_fact_synthesis.description: Combine multiple memories into a comprehensive answer

queries.query: Set up a new project for me
Write me a dev machine setup guide
Create a cultural taste profile for me
Draft a tech stack document for us
Write a dev process guide for our team
Write an intro about my photography hobby
Give me a business status briefing
Tell me about how the company was founded
Tell me about my small daily preferences

entity_disambiguation.description: Don't confuse other people's info with the user's info

queries.setup: My coworker uses Vim.
A friend is building an app with React Native.
queries.query: What do I use for frontend?

queries.setup: My sibling Kim Bada is a designer.
My teammate wants to use spaces.
A friend uses MySQL.
My colleague uses Ubuntu. Different from me.
My mentor uses Emacs.
Park Junseo went to Seoul National University.
Lee Soohyun is an AWS expert.
My coworker manages packages with npm.
A friend uses Jira for project management.
queries.query: What do I use for bug tracking?

queries.setup: A college buddy writes their blog on Medium.
queries.query: Where do I write my tech blog?

queries.setup: Seoyeon's bank account is at Toss Bank.
A friend just bought a Canon camera.
Lee Soohyun uses Docker.
My sibling uses a Samsung Galaxy.
My mentor uses zsh.
A teammate logs with winston.
Park Junseo is an ENFP.
Seoyeon likes lattes.

contradiction_direct.description: Detect explicit changes (with negation/transition words) and update

queries.update: Oh by the way, I switched to Cursor for my editor
I'm moving to Pangyo this month
I'm switching to GitKraken for Git now
Switched my terminal to Wezterm. More configurable
Moved documentation from Notion to Linear
queries.verify: What do we use for docs?

queries.update: Bumping my salary to 5 million won next month
I quit drinking beer. For health reasons
Switched to yarn. pnpm had compatibility issues
queries.verify: What package manager do I use?

queries.update: Moved to zsh. Fish didn't have enough plugins
Changed logging from pino to winston. Team is more familiar with it
queries.verify: What logging library do we use?

queries.update: Switched cameras to Fujifilm X-T5. Better colors than Sony
Moved blog from Hashnode to velog. Korean community is more active
queries.verify: Where do I write my tech blog?

queries.update: Switched to Arch Linux. More customizable than Fedora
Replaced DB with MySQL. Team has more experience with it
Switching frontend to Svelte. Next.js is too heavy
Switched to a Realforce keyboard. Happy Hacking was too small
Moved A/B testing to Amplitude. Better analytics than Posthog
queries.verify: What do we use for A/B testing?

queries.update: Now requiring 2 PR reviewers
queries.verify: How many PR reviewers are required?

queries.update: Switched monorepo tool to Nx. Turborepo was lacking features
queries.verify: What do we use for monorepo?

queries.update: Consolidated internal chat to Discord. Slack was too expensive

contradiction_indirect.description: Detect indirect changes (without negation) and track them

queries.update: Python's been fun again lately. Thinking of trying Django instead of FastAPI
queries.verify: What am I interested in lately?

queries.update: VS Code is surprisingly nice actually
queries.verify: How do I feel about editors lately?

queries.update: Haven't been going to the gym lately. Doing home workouts. Too busy
Been going to bed at 11 and waking up at 6 lately. Decided to sleep earlier
Started drinking lattes. Americanos were bothering my stomach
queries.verify: What coffee am I drinking these days?

queries.update: I've been really into Rust lately. The performance is amazing
Started yoga. Heard it's great for stretching
queries.verify: Any new exercise I've picked up?

queries.update: I started listening to podcasts. Tech podcasts during my commute
queries.verify: What do I do during commute?

queries.update: I've been going to a book club. Every Thursday
Got an iPad and started drawing. Using Procreate
I'm trying to eat more plant-based. For health
I downloaded a meditation app. Doing 10 minutes every morning
queries.verify: Anything new in my morning routine?

queries.update: I'm thinking about going hybrid for the company. Not fully remote
Seoyeon and I started taking cooking classes together. Italian course
queries.verify: Any activities I do with Seoyeon?

queries.update: Lately I've been walking Mungchi at the Han River a lot. Instead of running
queries.verify: What do I do at the Han River these days?

unchanged_persistence.description: Verify unchanged facts remain stable after other changes

queries.context: Other things changed but coffee preference stayed
Other tools changed but OS stayed

queries.query: Where did I go to college?

temporal.description: Accurately recognize time-related info and track change history

queries.query: What editor did I switch from?
Where did I used to live?
How did I originally use Git?
What terminal did I switch from?
When's the TIPS interim report due?
When was Naia OS v0.2 released?
When do you send investor reports?
What's the timeline from leaving Naver to founding the company?
When was the last server outage?
When did you last pull an all-nighter?
What's the Series A target date?
When's the mobile web launch deadline?
When did you do startup training?
What did you do last Christmas?
What did you do last Chuseok?

noise_resilience.description: Extract facts buried in casual chatter

queries.noisy_input: Ugh I'm so beat today. Lunch was decent, had sundaeguk. Oh right, I switched to an ultrawide monitor. 34 inches. Shipping took forever though.
Lol saw something hilarious on YouTube yesterday. Anyway, team dinner this Friday. We're getting chicken. Oh and I need to do code reviews.
queries.query: What's on the schedule this week?

queries.noisy_input: Yeah got it. Hang on. Hmm.. oh right I have an allergy. Shrimp allergy. Yeah yeah.
I'm so hungry. What should I eat for lunch. Oh also there's a presentation rehearsal tomorrow morning. At 10. Haven't made the slides yet lol
Whoa this is really good. Anyway I started studying English recently. Aiming for TOEIC 900. Ugh it's hard.
Haha nice. Oh right I've been looking into buying a car. Tesla Model 3 looks good. Pricey though. I'll get to it later.
queries.query: Am I thinking about getting a car?

queries.noisy_input: Met up with a friend yesterday, was hilarious. Oh and I started taking blood pressure medication. Doctor said to start early because of family history. Anyway yeah.
Yeah yeah got it. Also I'm going to Jeju next month. To visit my grandmother. Already booked the flight. What was I going to say...
Wow nice weather today. Oh right, we decided to raise subscription pricing. From 9,900 to 12,900 won. Was a tough call but had to be done.
Lol really? Anyway I tried Cursor AI and the copilot features are nice. Might use it as a secondary editor. What should I have for dinner.
queries.query: What did I say about Cursor?

queries.noisy_input: Sipping coffee while working and oh right, our monthly revenue broke 5 million won for the first time. Feels great. Ugh coffee's gone cold.
What was it again... oh right, I got Seoyeon AirPods Pro for her birthday. She loved them. Maybe I should get a pair too.
queries.query: What did you get Seoyeon for her birthday?

queries.noisy_input: Haha yeah sure. Oh and we passed the SOC2 Type 1 security audit. Finally. Took 3 months. Now gotta prep for Type 2.
Should I order delivery... oh right, new team member joined. Frontend developer Lee Jieun. 3 years experience. Came from Naver. No side dishes at home either.
Mungchi keeps barking... anyway we got a new GPU server. A100 80GB. For model training. Should take Mungchi for a walk.
Oh nice. Also code coverage hit 75% today. Finally broke past 70%. Just need a bit more to reach the 80% goal. Wanna go home.
Lol beats me. I've been trying the GTD methodology lately. Picked Todoist for task management. Starting with the free version. What should I eat for lunch.
queries.query: What do you use for task management?

queries.noisy_input: Had a phone call just now. Oh and we set this year's team goal to 200 DAU. Currently at 50 so need 4x growth. Ambitious haha.
Seriously? That's awesome. Oh right, had a designer interview and there was a good candidate. Clean portfolio. Name was Choi Yujin I think. Second interview next week.
So sleepy. Was coding until dawn yesterday. By the way, patent filing is complete. Application number is 2026-0012345. Attorney said it takes about 6 months.

before_after.description: Measure before implementation (baseline) and after with the same test set to prove improvement with metrics

report_format.comparison: Change rate across baseline vs after_mem0 vs after_p2
